title: Cieca litoralis
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Cieca litoralis". It is possible that it is a rare or unknown plant species.

However, I found some information about "Cicuta" plants, which might be the one you are referring to. Cicuta is a genus of plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae), commonly known as water hemlock.

Here's a paragraph about Cicuta litoralis (if this is the correct plant):

Cicuta litoralis is a perennial plant with a tall stem and compound leaves, native to coastal areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cicuta litoralis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cicuta litoralis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented due to potential toxicity.
